---
title: FORRT (Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training)
image: forrt.png
---

[FORRT (Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training)](https://forrt.org/) is a global initiative promoting open, transparent, and reproducible research practices through community-driven resources and training.

FLoRA (FORRT Library of Replication Attempts) is an open, curated database of replication studies, designed to improve the visibility and reuse of replication and reproduction research.

As part of a [FORRT hackathon](/community/index.qmd), I contributed to the development of the [flora-extractor tool](https://github.com/forrtproject/flora-extractor), which supports structured data extraction for FLoRA.

You can check out FLoRA here:

<iframe
  class="auto-preview"
  width="100%"
  height="800"
  src="https://forrt.org/flora-explorer/"
  title="FLoRA Explorer">
</iframe>
